title: Canonical JSON Shape and Requirement Grammar
purpose: Define the canonical JSON field model and requirement grammar used by the standard.
scope: This specification covers canonical JSON field names, requirement record shape, normative keyword usage, optional trace data, requirement title guidance, trace-field semantics, lineage and upstream-reference labels, inline identifier reference syntax inside JSON string fields, and the reference JSON templates for specification, architecture, work-item, and verification artifacts.
context: The standard needs predictable authored data, but the requirement itself should not be hidden under record-management boilerplate. Core JSON fields stay fixed, and repositories may add optional local extension fields when they do not redefine the core shape.

requirements.title: Keep specification JSON metadata document-level and compact
requirements.statement: Specification JSON artifacts MUST use the top-level fields `artifact_id`, `artifact_type`, `title`, `domain`, `capability`, `status`, and `owner`.
notes: `tags` and `related_artifacts` are optional.

requirements.title: Keep architecture JSON metadata focused on satisfaction links
requirements.statement: Architecture JSON artifacts MUST use the top-level fields `artifact_id`, `artifact_type`, `title`, `domain`, `status`, `owner`, and `satisfies`.
notes: `related_artifacts` is optional.

requirements.title: Keep work-item JSON metadata focused on implementation links
requirements.statement: Work-item JSON artifacts MUST use the top-level fields `artifact_id`, `artifact_type`, `title`, `domain`, `status`, `owner`, `addresses`, `design_links`, and `verification_links`.
notes: `related_artifacts` is optional.

requirements.title: Keep verification JSON metadata focused on proof links
requirements.statement: Verification JSON artifacts MUST use the top-level fields `artifact_id`, `artifact_type`, `title`, `domain`, `status`, `owner`, and `verifies`.
notes: `related_artifacts` is optional.
The `status` field is artifact-scoped; if the requirements in `verifies` do not share one outcome, split them into separate verification artifacts.

requirements.title: Use requirement records as the canonical entry point
requirements.statement: A canonical specification artifact MUST express requirements as records in its `requirements` collection, each with `id`, `title`, and `statement`.

requirements.title: Require BCP 14-style uppercase normative keywords in every clause
requirements.statement: The requirement clause MUST contain exactly one approved all-caps normative keyword.
notes: The standard uses BCP 14-style uppercase requirement language, inspired by RFC 2119 and RFC 8174.
Only uppercase forms carry the defined normative meaning; lowercase forms are plain English.
The approved keywords are `MUST`, `MUST NOT`, `SHALL`, `SHALL NOT`, `SHOULD`, `SHOULD NOT`, and `MAY`.
The keyword does not need to be the first word in the clause.

requirements.title: Make the compact clause the canonical requirement form
requirements.statement: The canonical requirement form MUST place the normative clause in the requirement record's `statement` field.
notes: Richer management metadata is allowed only as an optional local extension and should not obscure the clause itself.

requirements.title: Keep the requirement Trace block small and explicit
requirements.statement:
When a requirement includes structured trace data, it MUST use only the fields
`satisfied_by`, `implemented_by`, `verified_by`, `derived_from`,
`supersedes`, `upstream_refs`, and `related`.
notes: `derived_from` and `supersedes` are optional lineage fields.
`upstream_refs` is an optional free-form upstream source field.

requirements.title: Keep Trace and Notes optional
requirements.statement: The canonical requirement form MAY add optional `trace` and `notes` fields after the normative `statement` field.
notes: When lineage matters, prefer `Derived From`, `Supersedes`, and `Upstream Refs` over prose history.

requirements.title: Use backticks for inline identifier references
requirements.statement: Any inline reference to a canonical artifact identifier MUST use backticks.
notes: Inline identifier references can appear in requirement clauses, `Notes`, and other descriptive sections.
Use them for lightweight, human-readable cross-links such as component conformance, token usage, and cross-spec relationships.
Prefer structured trace fields when the relationship needs to be captured as explicit trace metadata.

requirements.title: Constrain inline identifier references to canonical artifact identifiers
requirements.statement: An inline identifier reference MUST identify a valid artifact identifier defined by the standard.
notes: The standard currently defines the `REQ-...`, `SPEC-...`, `ARC-...`, `WI-...`, and `VER-...` artifact families.
The identifier begins with the known prefix for its family and otherwise satisfies the identifier policy.
Inline identifier references are not file names, URLs, or loose prose labels.

requirements.title: Give requirement titles descriptive meaning
requirements.statement: The requirement title SHOULD name the obligation or concern in a short human-readable phrase rather than repeat the full clause or encode implementation detail.
notes: Requirement titles are scan aids, not the normative statement.
A title should help a reader recognize the subject of the requirement quickly.

requirements.title: Define trace label semantics by family
requirements.statement:
A requirement `trace` object MUST treat `satisfied_by`, `implemented_by`, and
`verified_by` as typed downstream trace links; `derived_from` and
`supersedes` as lineage; `upstream_refs` as upstream source citations; and
`related` as a loose association.
notes: The trace block records explicit relationships, while the surrounding prose may add context.
Inline identifier references stay separate from the trace block and do not change the meaning of its labels.
`Upstream Refs` may point at whole source documents or stable sub-document locations when a repository needs finer lineage to RFCs, policies, contracts, or similar material.

Generated evidence snapshots can carry tool-produced implementation
observations without turning those observations into canonical requirement
metadata.

requirements.title: Keep one specification and its requirements in the same file
requirements.statement: A specification artifact MUST represent one specification in one canonical `.json` file.

requirements.title: Keep related requirements under their specification
requirements.statement: A specification artifact MUST place one or more related `REQ-...` requirement records under that specification.

requirements.title: Keep work-item trace labels canonical
requirements.statement: A canonical work-item artifact MUST use the fields `addresses`, `design_links`, and `verification_links`.

requirements.title: Keep the supporting artifact templates predictable
requirements.statement: Architecture, work-item, and verification artifacts SHOULD follow the field order shown in the reference JSON templates so readers and tooling see a consistent structure.

requirements.title: Normalize normative keyword meaning
requirements.statement: Each approved normative keyword MUST have one defined meaning in the standard.

requirements.title: Define meaning of MUST
requirements.statement: This keyword MUST indicate a required condition.

requirements.title: Define meaning of MUST NOT
requirements.statement: This keyword MUST indicate a prohibited condition.

requirements.title: Define meaning of SHALL
requirements.statement: This keyword MUST indicate a required condition.

requirements.title: Define meaning of SHALL NOT
requirements.statement: This keyword MUST indicate a prohibited condition.

requirements.title: Define meaning of SHOULD
requirements.statement: This keyword MUST indicate a recommended but not strictly required condition.

requirements.title: Define meaning of MAY
requirements.statement: This keyword MUST indicate a permitted or optional condition.

requirements.title: Define meaning of SHOULD NOT
requirements.statement: This keyword MUST indicate a recommended-against condition.

requirements.title: Keep requirement clauses atomic and short
requirements.statement: A canonical requirement clause MUST express one obligation, rule, or constraint.

requirements.title: Keep requirement clauses concise by default
requirements.statement: A canonical requirement clause SHOULD remain a single sentence unless a short paragraph is required for precision.

requirements.title: Keep extension metadata optional and behind the clause
requirements.statement: Any local extension metadata MUST remain optional and follow the normative clause.

requirements.title: Keep front matter at document scope
requirements.statement: Canonical top-level metadata MUST describe the artifact as a whole rather than carry per-requirement metadata.

requirements.title: Keep generated evidence outside the canonical requirement trace
requirements.statement:
The standard MUST keep generated implementation evidence outside the canonical
requirement `Trace` block rather than embed tool-discovered test or code
observations directly in requirement text.

requirements.title: Allow inline identifier references in canonical JSON string fields
requirements.statement: A canonical JSON string field MAY include a backtick-delimited canonical identifier reference when the visible identifier text is the canonical token.
notes: Tooling should extract the visible identifier text rather than any projection-specific syntax layered on top of it.
This rule applies to canonical JSON string content, not only to derived browsing views.

requirements.title: Keep derived links precise when tooling adds navigation
requirements.statement: When tooling renders a derived navigation view that links to a specific requirement or other concrete subsection inside a canonical artifact, the link SHOULD include the relevant sub-document locator rather than point only at the containing file.
notes: Whole-document links remain appropriate when the target is the specification artifact as a whole.
This rule governs navigation precision in derived views, not identifier extraction from canonical JSON strings.
